content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 3, 1993

Diane M. Fishburn
Olson, Hagel, Fong, Leidig,
Waters & Fishburn
300 Capitol Mall, Ste. 350,
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-93-361

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1/ Because your question concerns conduct which has
already occurred, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

Your letter, dated September 24, 1993, accurately states my
advice that the committees or organizations supporting Measures N and
O and opposing Measures P and Q on the City of Orinda ballot for
the November 2, 1993, election had not received contributions and
had not incurred any filing obligations under the Act.

I also advised you that it appeared from the campaign filings
and other materials provided to us that the Orinda Sports and
Community Facilities Committee formed by Pacific New Wave
Corporation also had not qualified as a recipient committee under
Section 82013(a) due to the fact that all of the "contributions"
received by the committee were actually direct expenditures made
by the Pacific New Wave Corporation. (Craigie Advice Letter, No.
A-89-187.) I advised you that it was my opinion Pacific New Wave

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. References to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Diane M. Fishburn
Our File No. I-93-361
Page 2


Corporation should be filing as an independent expenditure
committee pursuant to Section 82013(b).

If you have any questions concerning this letter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether community organizations supporting or opposing ballot measures in Orinda had campaign finance filing obligations, and whether the Orion Sports and Community Facilities Committee qualified as a recipient committee or independent expenditure committee under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The community organizations supporting Measures N and O and opposing Measures P and Q had not received contributions and had no filing obligations. The Orion Sports and Community Facilities Committee did not qualify as a recipient committee because the funds received were direct expenditures by Pacific New Wave Corporation, and Pacific New Wave Corporation should instead be filing as an independent expenditure committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Confirmation letter of prior telephone advice treated as informal assistance under Regulation 18329(c). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present; information extracted from body of letter. References Craigie Advice Letter No. A-89-187. Letter dated November 3, 1993; incoming request dated September 24, 1993.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether community organizations supporting or opposing ballot measures in Orinda had campaign finance filing obligations, and whether the Orion Sports and Community Facilities Committee qualified as a recipient committee or independent expenditure committee under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The community organizations supporting Measures N and O and opposing Measures P and Q had not received contributions and had no filing obligations. The Orion Sports and Community Facilities Committee did not qualify as a recipient committee because the funds received were direct expenditures by Pacific New Wave Corporation, and Pacific New Wave Corporation should instead be filing as an independent expenditure committee.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirmation of prior telephone advice regarding campaign finance obligations for community organizations and committees involved in the November 2, 1993 Orinda municipal election ballot measures. The advice confirms that certain organizations had no filing obligations while Pacific New Wave Corporation should file as an independent expenditure committee.
